steps: Each horse requires 2 acres, so $h$ horses require $2h$ acres. Each goat requires 1 acre, so $g$ goats require $g$ acres.

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $g + h = 21$ — treats both animals as requiring 1 acre each, ignoring that horses need 2 acres. Incorrect.
  • B: $g + 2h = 21$ — equivalent to $2h + g = 21$, which matches the equation derived. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: $g + \frac{h}{2} = 21$ — gives horses only half an acre each instead of 2. Incorrect.
  • D: $\frac{g}{2} + h = 21$ — halves the goat acreage and gives each horse only 1 acre. Incorrect.
